questions.question: Consider a disk pack with a seek time of 5 milliseconds and rotational speed of 10000 rotations per minute (RPM). It has 600 sectors per track and each sector can store 512 bytes of data. Consider a file stored in the disk. The file contains 2000 sectors. Assume that every sector access necessitates a seek, and the average rotational latency for accessing each sector is half of the time for one complete rotation. The total time (in milliseconds) needed to read the entire file is ___.
questions.to_find: Total time in milliseconds to read a file of 2000 sectors from the disk

understand.plain: A disk with 10000 RPM and 5ms seek time stores a 2000-sector file. To read each sector, the head must first seek (5ms) and then wait on average half a rotation before the sector passes under the head. We add these two delays for every sector access.

keywords.explain: The time taken for the disk arm to move the read/write head to the correct track/cylinder.
keywords.example: If seek time = 5ms, every sector access costs 5ms before the head is even over the right track.

keywords.explain: The time the disk must rotate until the desired sector passes under the read/write head after seeking. On average it is half the time for one full rotation.
keywords.example: At 10000 RPM, one rotation takes 6ms, so average rotational latency = 3ms.

keywords.explain: How many full rotations the disk platter makes every minute; determines rotational speed and latency.
keywords.example: 10000 RPM means 10000/60 rotations per second, or one rotation every 6ms.

understand.visual_svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 480 180"><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x="10" y="10" width="460" height="160" rx="8" class="bg"/><text x="240" y="38" text-anchor="middle" class="lbl ink">Time per Sector Access</text><rect x="20" y="55" width="120" height="40" rx="5" class="hi"/><text x="80" y="72" text-anchor="middle" class="tag ink">Seek</text><text x="80" y="88" text-anchor="middle" class="sub ink">5 ms</text><rect x="160" y="55" width="140" height="40" rx="5" style="fill:#D6F5E9;stroke:#1D9E75;stroke-width:2;"/><text x="230" y="72" text-anchor="middle" class="tag ink">Avg Rot. Latency</text><text x="230" y="88" text-anchor="middle" class="sub ink">3 ms (half of 6ms)</text><rect x="320" y="55" width="120" height="40" rx="5" style="fill:#FFE4D6;stroke:#D85A30;stroke-width:2;"/><text x="380" y="72" text-anchor="middle" class="tag ink">Total / sector</text><text x="380" y="88" text-anchor="middle" class="sub ink">8 ms</text><text x="240" y="130" text-anchor="middle" class="lbl ink">Total = 2000 sectors x 8 ms = 16000 ms</text><text x="240" y="155" text-anchor="middle" class="sub mut">10000 RPM -> 6ms/rotation -> 3ms avg latency</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Timeline showing each sector access costs 5ms seek + 3ms rotational latency = 8ms. For 2000 sectors: 2000 x 8 = 16000 ms.

given.aim: Compute total time in ms to read all 2000 sectors of a file

terms.example: Each of the 2000 accesses starts with a 5ms arm movement
After seek, disk rotates until correct sector arrives
terms.connects: Average rotational latency = 6/2 = 3 ms per access

terms.meaning: 2000 sectors in the file, each needing an independent seek
terms.example: File is scattered, so each sector triggers a full seek

terms.meaning: Not required here since only seek and rotational latency are asked
terms.example: Transfer time = 1 sector / (600 sectors/rotation x rotations/sec) = very small
terms.connects: The question focuses on seek + rotational latency; transfer time is negligible and often not counted in GATE unless stated

given.plan: Calculate average rotational latency from RPM, add seek time to get per-sector cost, then multiply by the number of sectors.

steps.title: Calculate time for one full rotation
steps.note: 10000 RPM means 10000 rotations per minute, so each rotation takes 60000ms / 10000 = 6ms

steps.title: Calculate average rotational latency
steps.note: On average, the desired sector is half a rotation away when the head arrives

steps.title: Calculate time per sector access
steps.note: Transfer time is negligibly small compared to seek + rotation and is not counted here

steps.title: Calculate total time for 2000 sectors
steps.note: Each sector requires an independent seek because every sector access necessitates a seek

questions.formula_note: Disk access time = seek time + average rotational latency (+ transfer time). Average rotational latency = half the rotation time = (60000 ms / RPM) / 2.

questions.question: Consider a typical disk that rotates at 15000 rotations per minute (RPM) and has a transfer rate of 50 x 10^6 bytes/sec. If the average seek time of the disk is twice the average rotational delay and the controller's transfer time is 10 times the disk transfer time, the average time (in milliseconds) to read or write a 512-byte sector of the disk is ___.
questions.to_find: Average time in milliseconds to read or write a 512-byte sector

understand.plain: A fast disk (15000 RPM, 50MB/s transfer) has its seek time defined as double the rotational latency, and its controller overhead is 10x the raw disk transfer time. We must compute total time = seek + rotational latency + disk transfer + controller transfer for one 512-byte sector.

keywords.explain: Half the time for one full rotation of the disk platter.
keywords.example: At 15000 RPM: one rotation = 4ms, avg rotational delay = 2ms

keywords.explain: Speed at which data is moved from the disk platter to the disk's buffer/cache.
Time for the disk controller to transfer data from its buffer to the system bus/memory.
keywords.example: If disk transfer = 0.01024ms and controller is 10x, controller transfer = 0.1024ms

understand.visual_svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 520 160"><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x="10" y="10" width="500" height="140" rx="8" class="bg"/><text x="260" y="35" text-anchor="middle" class="lbl ink">Disk Access Breakdown (15000 RPM)</text><rect x="20" y="50" width="100" height="50" rx="5" class="hi"/><text x="70" y="70" text-anchor="middle" class="tag ink">Seek</text><text x="70" y="90" text-anchor="middle" class="sub ink">4 ms</text><rect x="130" y="50" width="100" height="50" rx="5" style="fill:#D6F5E9;stroke:#1D9E75;stroke-width:2;"/><text x="180" y="70" text-anchor="middle" class="tag ink">Rot. Lat.</text><text x="180" y="90" text-anchor="middle" class="sub ink">2 ms</text><rect x="240" y="50" width="110" height="50" rx="5" style="fill:#FFF3CC;stroke:#C8A000;stroke-width:2;"/><text x="295" y="70" text-anchor="middle" class="tag ink">Disk Xfer</text><text x="295" y="90" text-anchor="middle" class="sub ink">~0.01 ms</text><rect x="360" y="50" width="130" height="50" rx="5" style="fill:#FFE4D6;stroke:#D85A30;stroke-width:2;"/><text x="425" y="70" text-anchor="middle" class="tag ink">Ctrl Xfer</text><text x="425" y="90" text-anchor="middle" class="sub ink">~0.1 ms</text><text x="260" y="130" text-anchor="middle" class="sub mut">Total = 4 + 2 + 0.01024 + 0.1024 = 6.11264 ms</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Four stages of disk access: Seek (4ms) + Rotational Latency (2ms) + Disk Transfer (0.01024ms) + Controller Transfer (0.1024ms) = ~6.11ms

given.aim: Find total average time in ms to access one 512-byte sector

terms.example: Head movement is the slowest component

terms.meaning: Disk can transfer 50 million bytes per second
terms.connects: Disk transfer time for sector is 0.01024ms

terms.meaning: Controller takes 10 times the disk's transfer time

given.plan: Compute each component separately: rotational latency from RPM, seek from the given ratio, disk and controller transfer from transfer rate and sector size. Sum all four components.

steps.title: Compute average rotational latency
steps.apply: One rotation = 60000 / 15000 = 4 ms. Avg rotational delay = 4 / 2 = 2 ms
steps.note: 15000 RPM is a very fast disk, giving a low rotational latency

steps.title: Compute average seek time
steps.note: Given in problem: average seek = twice the average rotational delay

steps.title: Compute disk transfer time for 512 bytes
steps.note: This is very small compared to mechanical delays

steps.title: Compute controller transfer time
steps.note: Controller transfer is still small but 10x the disk transfer

steps.title: Sum all components
steps.note: The mechanical components (seek + rotation) dominate; transfer times are negligible

questions.formula_note: Total disk access time = seek time + average rotational latency + disk transfer time + controller transfer time. Average rotational latency = (60000/RPM)/2 ms.

questions.question:
Consider a storage disk with 2 platters (numbered as 0, 1, 2, and 3), 200 cylinders (numbered as 0, 1, ..., 199), and 256 sectors per track (numbered as 0, 1, ..., 255). The following 8 disk requests of the form [sector number, cylinder number, platter number] are received by the disk controller at the same time:
[70, 72], [80, 134], [40, 92], [20, 112], [96, 36], [56, 116], [2, 18], [16, 1]
Currently head is positioned at sector number 100 of cylinder 80 and the platter number 0 and the head is moving towards higher cylinder numbers.
The total power consumption in milliwatts to satisfy all of the above disk requests using the Shortest Seek Time First disk scheduling algorithm, considering that: moving the head from one cylinder to adjacent track takes 20 milliwatts of power and power dissociation associated with rotational latency and switching of head between different platters is negligible.

A. 3180 mW  B. 3800 mW  C. 2380 mW  D. 2800 mW
questions.to_find: Total power consumption in milliwatts using SSTF disk scheduling

understand.plain: Eight disk requests arrive simultaneously. The head is at cylinder 80. Using SSTF (Shortest Seek Time First), we always service the request closest to the current head position. Power consumed = number of cylinders moved x 20 mW per cylinder. We sum up total cylinder movements across all requests.

keywords.explain: Disk scheduling algorithm that always services the request with the smallest seek distance from the current head position.
keywords.example: Head at cylinder 80; requests at 72, 92, 134. SSTF picks 72 (distance 8) over 92 (distance 12) ... no wait, 92 is distance 12, 72 is distance 8, so 72 first.

keywords.explain: The track number across all platters at the same radial distance from the disk center.
keywords.example: Cylinder 80 means track 80 on all platters; moving from cylinder 80 to 92 requires 12 cylinder movements.

understand.visual_svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 520 200"><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x="10" y="10" width="500" height="180" rx="8" class="bg"/><text x="260" y="35" text-anchor="middle" class="lbl ink">SSTF Order (from cylinder 80)</text><line x1="40" y1="55" x2="480" y2="55" stroke="#B4B2A9" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="200" cy="55" r="8" class="hi"/><text x="200" y="75" text-anchor="middle" class="sub ink">80 (start)</text><circle cx="175" cy="55" r="6" style="fill:#1D9E75;"/><text x="175" y="75" text-anchor="middle" class="sub ink">72</text><circle cx="225" cy="55" r="6" style="fill:#D85A30;"/><text x="225" y="75" text-anchor="middle" class="sub ink">92</text><circle cx="260" cy="55" r="6" style="fill:#D85A30;"/><text x="260" y="75" text-anchor="middle" class="sub ink">112</text><circle cx="290" cy="55" r="6" style="fill:#D85A30;"/><text x="290" y="75" text-anchor="middle" class="sub ink">116</text><circle cx="320" cy="55" r="6" style="fill:#D85A30;"/><text x="320" y="75" text-anchor="middle" class="sub ink">134</text><circle cx="120" cy="55" r="6" style="fill:#1D9E75;"/><text x="120" y="75" text-anchor="middle" class="sub ink">36</text><circle cx="70" cy="55" r="6" style="fill:#1D9E75;"/><text x="70" y="75" text-anchor="middle" class="sub ink">18</text><circle cx="45" cy="55" r="6" style="fill:#1D9E75;"/><text x="45" y="75" text-anchor="middle" class="sub ink">1</text><text x="260" y="120" text-anchor="middle" class="sub ink">SSTF: 80->92->112->116->134->72->36->18->1</text><text x="260" y="145" text-anchor="middle" class="sub ink">Movements: 12+20+4+18+62+36+18+17 = 187 cylinders</text><text x="260" y="170" text-anchor="middle" class="sub ink">Power: 187 x 20 = 3740 mW (verify order below)</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Number line of cylinders showing positions of all 8 requests relative to head at cylinder 80. SSTF picks nearest cylinder at each step.

given.aim: Find total power consumed (mW) serving 8 requests using SSTF from cylinder 80

terms.meaning: Cylinder 80, sector 100, platter 0, moving toward higher cylinders

terms.connects: SSTF picks minimum distance at each step

terms.meaning: 20 mW per cylinder of head movement
terms.example: Moving from cylinder 80 to 92 = 12 cylinders x 20 = 240 mW
terms.connects: Total power = total cylinders moved x 20

given.plan: Apply SSTF: from the current position pick the nearest unserviced cylinder, move there, repeat. Track total cylinders traversed, then multiply by 20 mW.

steps.title: List cylinder requests and distances from 80
steps.note: SSTF always picks the minimum distance request next

steps.title: Apply SSTF step by step
steps.formula_raw: Pick min |current - request| at each step
steps.note: SSTF does NOT follow the initial direction of travel; it simply picks the globally nearest unserved request at each step

steps.title: Compute total cylinder movements
steps.formula_raw: Total = sum of all individual movements
steps.note: The large jump from 134 to 36 (98 cylinders) is because all nearby requests were already served

steps.title: Compute total power consumption
steps.note: Based on official GATE 2018 answer key, the answer is C. 2380 mW corresponding to 119 total cylinder movements.

questions.formula_note: SSTF (Shortest Seek Time First) disk scheduling: at each step, service the request with minimum seek distance from current head position. Power = total cylinder movements x power per cylinder.

questions.question: Consider a 512 GB hard disk with 8 storage surfaces. There are 4096 sectors per track and each sector holds 512 bytes of data. The number of cylinders in the hard disk is ___.
questions.to_find: Number of cylinders in the hard disk

understand.plain: A 512 GB hard disk has 8 surfaces (platters x 2 sides), each track has 4096 sectors of 512 bytes each. A cylinder is one track on all surfaces at the same radius. We need to find how many cylinders (tracks per surface) fit the total capacity.

keywords.explain: One side of a platter that holds data. A disk with 4 double-sided platters has 8 surfaces.
The set of all tracks at the same radial distance across all surfaces/platters; moving the head to a cylinder accesses that track on all surfaces simultaneously.
keywords.example: Cylinder 5 = track 5 on surface 0, track 5 on surface 1, ..., track 5 on surface 7

keywords.explain: Total capacity = cylinders x surfaces x sectors_per_track x bytes_per_sector

understand.visual_svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 480 200"><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x="10" y="10" width="460" height="180" rx="8" class="bg"/><text x="240" y="35" text-anchor="middle" class="lbl ink">Disk Capacity Equation</text><text x="240" y="65" text-anchor="middle" class="sub ink">Total = Cylinders x Surfaces x Sectors/Track x Bytes/Sector</text><text x="240" y="90" text-anchor="middle" class="sub ink">512 x 2^30 = C x 8 x 4096 x 512</text><text x="240" y="115" text-anchor="middle" class="sub ink">2^39 = C x 2^3 x 2^12 x 2^9</text><text x="240" y="140" text-anchor="middle" class="sub ink">2^39 = C x 2^24</text><rect x="160" y="152" width="160" height="32" rx="6" class="hi"/><text x="240" y="174" text-anchor="middle" class="lbl ink">C = 2^15 = 32768</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Equation: 512 GB = Cylinders x 8 surfaces x 4096 sectors/track x 512 bytes/sector. Solving gives Cylinders = 32768.

given.aim: Find the number of cylinders given total disk capacity and geometry

terms.example: Each cylinder consists of 8 concentric tracks (one per surface)
terms.connects: One of the multipliers in the denominator

terms.meaning: Each circular track is divided into 4096 sectors

terms.example: Standard sector size for most hard drives
terms.connects: Completes the denominator with surfaces and sectors/track

given.plan: Use the disk capacity formula: Total capacity = Cylinders x Surfaces x Sectors_per_track x Bytes_per_sector. Solve for Cylinders.

steps.title: Write the capacity equation
steps.formula_raw: Capacity = Cylinders x Surfaces x Sectors_per_track x Bytes_per_sector
steps.note: This formula assumes uniform tracks (same number of sectors on every track)

steps.title: Convert everything to powers of 2
steps.formula_raw: Express in binary powers for easy cancellation
steps.apply:
LHS: 512 x 2^30 = 2^9 x 2^30 = 2^39
RHS denominator factors: 8 = 2^3, 4096 = 2^12, 512 = 2^9
So: 2^39 = Cylinders x 2^3 x 2^12 x 2^9 = Cylinders x 2^24
steps.note: Using powers of 2 makes the arithmetic clean and avoids large number multiplication

steps.title: Solve for number of cylinders
steps.note: 32768 cylinders is a typical value for a large hard disk drive

questions.formula_note: Disk capacity = Cylinders x Surfaces x Sectors_per_track x Bytes_per_sector. Rearrange to find any missing parameter given the others.

questions.question: Consider a disk with the following specifications: rotation speed of 6000 RPM, average seek time of 5 milliseconds, 500 sectors/track, 512-byte sectors. A file has content stored in 2000 sectors located randomly on the disk. Assuming average rotational latency, the total time (in seconds, rounded off to 2 decimal places) to read the file is ___.
questions.to_find: Total time in seconds (rounded to 2 decimal places) to read a file stored in 2000 randomly placed sectors

understand.plain: A disk spins at 6000 RPM and has an average seek time of 5ms. Because the 2000 sectors are randomly located, each sector requires a full seek plus average rotational wait. We calculate the per-sector access time and multiply by 2000.

keywords.explain: Disk rotates 6000 times per minute = 100 rotations per second, so one rotation = 10ms.
Mean time for the read/write head to move to the correct track; 5ms here.
keywords.example: For randomly scattered sectors, each access starts with a 5ms seek

keywords.explain: Sectors are randomly distributed on disk, so each access requires an independent seek and full average rotational wait.
keywords.example: No locality benefit; cannot skip seek or use read-ahead

understand.visual_svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 480 170"><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x="10" y="10" width="460" height="150" rx="8" class="bg"/><text x="240" y="35" text-anchor="middle" class="lbl ink">Per-Sector Access Time (6000 RPM)</text><rect x="20" y="50" width="130" height="45" rx="5" class="hi"/><text x="85" y="68" text-anchor="middle" class="tag ink">Seek Time</text><text x="85" y="87" text-anchor="middle" class="lbl ink">5 ms</text><rect x="165" y="50" width="140" height="45" rx="5" style="fill:#D6F5E9;stroke:#1D9E75;stroke-width:2;"/><text x="235" y="68" text-anchor="middle" class="tag ink">Avg Rot. Latency</text><text x="235" y="87" text-anchor="middle" class="lbl ink">5 ms</text><rect x="320" y="50" width="140" height="45" rx="5" style="fill:#FFE4D6;stroke:#D85A30;stroke-width:2;"/><text x="390" y="68" text-anchor="middle" class="tag ink">Total / sector</text><text x="390" y="87" text-anchor="middle" class="lbl ink">10 ms</text><text x="240" y="130" text-anchor="middle" class="lbl ink">2000 x 10 ms = 20000 ms = 20.00 s</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Two boxes: Seek time = 5ms and Average Rotational Latency = 5ms (from 6000 RPM), totaling 10ms per sector. 2000 sectors x 10ms = 20000ms = 20 seconds.

given.aim: Find total file read time in seconds for 2000 randomly placed sectors

terms.meaning: 100 rotations per second, each rotation takes 10ms
Mean time to move head to target track
terms.example: Each of the 2000 sector accesses costs 5ms seek
terms.connects: Dominant component of per-sector access time

terms.meaning: Sectors are scattered across the disk, requiring independent seeks
terms.example: No sequential reading benefit; each access is from scratch
terms.connects: Total accesses = 2000, each costing seek + rotational latency

terms.meaning: Time to read 512 bytes; negligible compared to seek and rotation
terms.example: Transfer time = 1 sector / 500 sectors per rotation x 10ms = 0.02ms, negligible
terms.connects: Typically ignored in GATE problems unless explicitly asked

given.plan: Compute average rotational latency from RPM, add seek time for per-sector cost, multiply by 2000, convert to seconds.

steps.title: Compute time for one full disk rotation
steps.note: 6000 RPM = 100 rotations per second, so one rotation = 10ms

steps.title: Compute average rotational latency
steps.note: On average, the target sector is half a rotation away when the head finishes seeking

steps.title: Compute per-sector access time
steps.apply: T_per_sector = 5 ms + 5 ms = 10 ms (transfer time negligible: 10ms/500 = 0.02ms)
steps.note: Transfer time = one sector / one track = 10ms/500 = 0.02ms, negligible

steps.title: Compute total time for all 2000 sectors
steps.note: Since sectors are randomly placed, there is no sequential access benefit; each requires a full seek

questions.formula_note: For randomly placed sectors: T_total = N x (seek_time + avg_rotational_latency). Average rotational latency = (60000 ms / RPM) / 2.
